summary: Justin conducts a live broker call with Zachary in Charlotte, NC regarding a 16-unit value-add property and discusses deal sourcing strategy, local lender options, and property manager recommendations. While directly relevant to Brian's deal sourcing challenge, the session focuses primarily on relationship-building mechanics rather than deal-specific analysis or underwriting frameworks.
takeaways: Call brokers on specific deals first to establish relationships, then ask 'do you have any others you're working on BOVs for' to uncover pre-market opportunities
Face-to-face market visits convert phone relationships into trusted local connections - schedule in-person meetings when traveling to a market
For 10-30 unit deals under $3.2M, target lenders offering bridge-to-permanent products (bridge at acquisition + renovation, roll to permanent at 85% occupancy with no rollover fees)
Local property managers that traditionally handled single-family but want to enter multifamily offer better terms and service than firms specializing exclusively in multifamily

When asking brokers for recommendations, use tactful framing: 'what are some property managers your clients use' rather than 'who do you recommend' to get honest intel without putting them on the spot

skill_candidates.change: Implement specific conversation flow: lead with current deal intel, transition to 'any BOVs in progress,' then schedule face-to-face meeting during market visits
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin demonstrates call structure with Zachary: opens discussing 16-unit property details, pivots to pipeline questions ('any BOVs out on with sellers'), closes by arranging Monday/Tuesday in-person meeting. He explains: 'face to face is so powerful, especially when you're out of area' and notes that brokers value knowing you personally.

skill_candidates.change: Estimate value-add capex by unit economics: 16-unit C-class property with recent renovation likely carries $20k-25k per unit in improvements ($320k-400k total)
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin reverse-engineers the Cedarhurst deal: 'they probably have at least 20,000 a unit into this thing between the roof and the siding and the windows, the interiors... somewhere between 3 to 400 grand into this property.' Demonstrates how to estimate renovation spend from property condition.

skill_candidates.change: Create landing page + pitch deck + summary video (5-8 min) as proof-of-execution before pursuing deals; send to 20-30 person database via email with deal link, PDF, and 'who do you know' ask
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin reviews Kelly's completed mock deal package and emphasizes: 'you've already gone through preseason, you're ready for opening day.' He explains the email should say 'closing in 60-90 days, here's summary/pitch deck/landing page link, who do you know.' This preseason work builds broker confidence.

title: Weekly Coaching -03-26-26 | Justin Brennan
